proposed_program_information.program_description:
This gentle yoga and mindful movement series is designed for adults who are looking to build strength, improve flexibility, and reduce stress in a supportive, welcoming environment—no prior yoga experience required.

Over the course of the session, participants will be guided through a combination of breathwork, basic yoga postures, and simple mobility exercises that can be adapted for a wide range of bodies and abilities. Each class will begin with a short check-in and grounding practice, move into a slow-flow sequence focused on safe alignment and joint stability, and end with restorative poses and a brief guided relaxation or meditation.

The emphasis of this program is on listening to your own body, moving at a sustainable pace, and learning tools you can take home to support everyday wellness. We will cover:
- Foundational standing, seated, and lying-down poses
- Safe modifications for tight hamstrings, sensitive knees, and low back issues
- Gentle core strengthening to support posture
- Breathing techniques to help manage anxiety and improve focus
- Simple stretches and mini-routines you can do at your desk or in your living room

Participants are encouraged to work at their own comfort level—many postures can be done with the support of a chair or wall. This is a co-ed, body-positive class, welcoming beginners as well as those returning to movement after time away. Please wear comfortable clothing you can move in and bring a water bottle. Mats and a limited number of props will be available, though participants are welcome to bring their own if they prefer.
proposed_program_information.age_or_grade_of_participants: Adults, ages 25–65 (flexible for motivated participants slightly outside this range)
proposed_program_information.ideal_type_of_space: Quiet, clean indoor space with smooth flooring suitable for yoga mats (wood, laminate, or low-pile carpet). Room should comfortably fit up to 18 mats with space to move around, ideally 800–1,000 square feet. Access to a thermostat or reasonable temperature control is helpful, as participants will be barefoot and on the floor. Dimmable or at least adjustable lighting is preferred for relaxation at the end of class. A small storage area or closet to keep a bin of yoga props (blocks, straps, extra mats, and blankets) on-site between classes would be ideal. Access to nearby restrooms and drinking water is important. A Bluetooth-capable speaker or ability to play soft background music from a phone or laptop would be a bonus but is not strictly required.
proposed_program_information.equipment_needed_description:
Participants should bring:
- A yoga mat (non-slip if possible). I will have 5–6 extra mats available for those who do not own one.
- A reusable water bottle with a secure lid.
- A light sweater or sweatshirt and socks for the relaxation portion at the end of class, as some participants tend to get chilly when not moving.

Optional but recommended if they already own them:
- A yoga block or two
- A yoga strap or a long belt
- A small blanket or towel to provide extra cushioning for knees or to use under the head.

instructorfacilitator_information.instructor_bio:
Laura Chen is a Williston-based yoga instructor, movement educator, and working mom who is passionate about making mindful movement accessible to everyday people with real-life schedules and real-life bodies. After discovering yoga in her late twenties as a way to manage chronic neck and shoulder tension from office work, Laura experienced firsthand how a consistent, gentle practice could reduce pain, improve sleep, and support mental well-being.

Over the past decade, she has taught in a variety of settings—including local studios, workplace wellness programs, and small community groups—before focusing more intentionally on neighborhood-based classes for adults who may feel intimidated by traditional studio environments. Laura’s teaching style is calm, clear, and down-to-earth. She places a strong emphasis on safe alignment, options for different bodies, and cultivating a non-judgmental atmosphere where participants feel comfortable asking questions, taking breaks, and adapting poses.

Outside of teaching, Laura works part-time as a communications consultant and spends much of her free time hiking local trails with her husband, their 9-year-old daughter, and their very energetic rescue dog, Milo. She has lived in Chittenden County for over 12 years and is excited to partner with the Williston Recreation & Parks Department to offer an approachable, community-centered yoga series close to home.
instructorfacilitator_information.instructor_qualifications:
• Yoga Teacher Training & Certifications:
  - Registered Yoga Teacher (RYT-200) through Yoga Alliance, completed at Laughing River Yoga in Burlington, VT (2017). Training included 200+ hours of instruction in anatomy, alignment, teaching methodology, yoga philosophy, and hands-on assisting.
  - 40-hour Trauma-Informed Yoga Training with the Center for Trauma & Embodiment (2020), focusing on creating emotionally safe, choice-based classes and understanding the impact of trauma on the nervous system.
  - 20-hour Yoga for Healthy Aging workshop series with Baxter Bell, MD, and Melina Meza (online, 2021), covering joint health, balance, bone strength, and modifications for common age-related conditions.

• Teaching Experience:
  - 7+ years of teaching yoga and mindful movement to adults at various levels, from complete beginners to experienced practitioners.
  - Former group class instructor at two Burlington-area studios (2017–2022), teaching beginner yoga, gentle flow, and restorative classes.
  - Experience running small-group workplace wellness classes for local businesses, including a 6-week “Deskworkers’ Yoga” series for a marketing firm in South Burlington.
  - Led multiple 6–8 week beginner series in community settings, including a pilot program for parents through a local elementary school PTO.

• Relevant Education & Professional Background:
  - B.A. in Communications, University of Massachusetts Amherst (2010).
  - Part-time communications consultant, which has helped develop clear verbal and written communication skills, useful for explaining alignment cues and modifications in an accessible way.

• Additional Skills & Safety Training:
  - Current Adult CPR/AED and Basic First Aid certification through the American Red Cross (most recently renewed 2024-03-15).
  - Familiarity with common musculoskeletal concerns such as low back pain, tight hamstrings, and knee sensitivity, and experience offering chair-based or wall-supported options.
  - Comfortable collaborating with participants who may be under the care of a physical therapist, and experienced in encouraging them to stay within their provider’s guidelines.

Overall, my qualifications combine formal yoga training, substantial hands-on teaching experience, and a strong focus on accessibility, safety, and clear communication—well-suited to a community recreation setting that welcomes adults with a wide range of abilities and backgrounds.
